ops.title: Cabin fit semantics and constraint gating design
ops.body:
# Cabin fit semantics and constraint gating design

Decisions embedded in the W1/W2 quality-restoration waves (commits a6b5efaa "feat(gates)" and 699098d8 "feat(rank)"), each deliberate — do not "fix" them back:

## Cabin semantics
- **Cabin comparisons are per-segment, never leg-level.** A leg's top-level `cabin` is not one representation: normal award legs carry letters ("J"), stitched award-onward legs carry the onward-minimum's *name* ("economy"), mixed legs can carry "MIXED". Every consumer that ranks cabins must iterate `detail["segments"][*]["cabin"]` (uniformly letters) via `constants.cabin_rank`. The pre-fix leg-level read crashed rank on award-onward hybrids under a cabin constraint.
- **`fit._cabin_fit` counts only below-preferred minutes** (`below_cabin_minutes`; the old `mixed_cabin_minutes` counted *any* differing cabin, so a first-class segment scored as a miss against a business preference — a real bug that would have poisoned the primary-clears guard). Higher cabins are zero miss; `matched` still means all-segments-exactly-preferred.
- **`constraints.cabin` gates award legs only; cash legs are exempt by design.** Bridge quotes are economy by construction — gating them would delete every hybrid under a business constraint.

## Constraint gating in rank()
- Only *confirmed constraints* and vetoes drop journeys (doctrine: preferences never gate). Gates live in rank()'s journey loop beside `_mileage_limit`, each disclosed in `dropped` with `{journey_id, reason}`.
- **`prefs.avoid_transit`** vetoes at composition (`journeys._compose` → `gated`): transit points are *both* sides of every within-leg segment boundary plus landside self-transfer boundaries between consecutive legs; journey origin, final destination, and the round-trip turnaround never count.

## Primary-clears guard + trigger (W2)
- Primary-code participation is **per-code**: `judgment.factors` override (factor-granular, speaks for all the factor's codes) > the code's own declared `preferences[code].priority` > registry default. The strongest-of-several factor fold exists *only* for the verdict lane (`_tiers`/`derive_profile`) — reusing it for guard codes escalates a note-declared sibling (confirmed by counterexample).
- The clears guard and the notable trigger read only deterministic `journey["preference_misses"]`; the trigger additionally skips journeys the **verify artifact** marks gone/degraded (`facts["availability_verification"]` — assess verdicts on `availability_verified` are rejected at validation, so the signal cannot be LLM-authored).

## Cross-language sync
- `NOTABLE_PREFERENCE_STRETCH_LIMIT` (constants.py) must stay in sync with the "up to 2" literal in `skills/getaway/plan-trip.js`'s assess prompt; the assess-artifact validator enforces the Python side.
ops.when: before modifying cabin preference evaluation, constraint gating in rank(), avoid_transit behavior, or the notable-stretch trigger
